compiled_state.title: [fix-bug skill]: orchestrator invokes Skill("review") -> built-in PR-review skill loads instead of dso review flow
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

When running /dso:fix-bug, the orchestrator reached the commit-workflow step and tried to invoke the DSO code-review flow via the Skill tool. The DSO project does not expose a '/dso:review' skill — instead, /dso:commit internally invokes REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md. In practice, the CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table lists '/dso:review' as 'Code review via sub-agent', which creates an implicit expectation that it exists.

## Observed Behavior

- Orchestrator called Skill(skill='review') from within /dso:fix-bug commit flow
- The built-in Claude Code /review skill loaded (PR-review for GitHub: runs 'gh pr list', 'gh pr view <number>', 'gh pr diff <number>')
- The built-in /review is the WRONG flow for an in-progress local commit; it expects a PR number and operates on remote state
- No warning or redirect to the DSO code-review workflow was emitted

## Expected Behavior

Either:
(a) '/dso:review' should exist as a first-class skill that wraps REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and dispatches dso:code-reviewer-* sub-agents, OR
(b) CLAUDE.md Quick Reference and fix-bug/commit skill guidance should be updated to explicitly state 'review happens inline via REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md — no Skill tool call', so orchestrators read the workflow file instead of reaching for Skill('review').

## Actual Impact

Orchestrator loses context attempting to use the built-in skill, then has to fall back to reading REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and dispatching reviewers manually. Wastes tokens and risks the orchestrator silently using the built-in PR-review flow on a local change (producing a review of wrong state).

## Reproduce

1. Start /dso:fix-bug on any bug where a code fix is applied
2. Reach the commit-workflow step that requires review
3. Orchestrator invokes Skill(skill='review')
4. The built-in /review skill loads (PR number: prompt) instead of the DSO review flow

## Root Cause Hypothesis (pre-investigation)

CLAUDE.md Quick Reference at row 'Code review via sub-agent | /dso:review | /dso:plan-review' implies a /dso:review skill exists. The DSO plugin registers many dso:* skills but no dso:review. The Skill tool fuzzy-matches 'review' to the built-in review skill (not the DSO namespace). This creates a silent routing failure where the orchestrator believes it has invoked the DSO flow but has actually invoked the built-in PR-review flow.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode): bug targets an LLM-behavioral skill/agent/prompt file. Per CLAUDE.md rule 22 and behavioral-testing-standard Rule 5, any modification requires dedicated bot-psychologist investigation and explicit user approval. DEFERRED: batch-escalating to end-of-session user review; no code change applied this session.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation (cluster C, sonnet, KERNEL): Taxonomy #10 Brittle API Mapping + #3 Silent Instruction Truncation. ROOT_CAUSE: CLAUDE.md Quick Reference row maps `/dso:review` — but no `dso:review` skill exists in the registered skill list. When fix-bug's commit path calls `Skill("review")`, the Skill tool silently matches the built-in `/review` (GitHub PR review), not the DSO workflow. Quick Reference shorthand is high-locality (read early); COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md redirect to REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md is lower-locality (only reached on full workflow read). PROPOSED_FIXES: (a) CLAUDE.md Quick Reference: replace `/dso:review` row with "Read plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md inline (no /dso:review skill exists; built-in /review is WRONG flow)". (b) CLAUDE.md Always Do These rule 4: change to "read REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md inline. Do NOT call Skill('review')". (c) fix-bug SKILL.md commit-path step: add prohibition on Skill("review"). Note: further verification advised — /dso:review may actually exist as a plugin skill invocation path. Confidence: medium. Minimal-fix applied: true.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_APPROVAL (2026-04-20): User directive — 'Fixes recommended by bot-psychologist are approved' for all Category D intent-confirmed clusters. Cluster C fix approved WITH REVIEW CAVEAT: proposed fixes assume /dso:review is non-existent skill — requires verification in next session before applying (/dso:review IS successfully invoked at this session's start, so the bot-psych analysis may be wrong for this specific repo). Safer fix: add fix-bug SKILL.md commit-path prohibition on Skill("review") to force explicit REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md inline read.
comments.author: Test
